doc: Format a single `Cell` IR entry into its `.cell` text representation.
Fixes the double-`\n` bug: a `%ENDBLOCK` line is emitted with exactly one
trailing newline; no extra newline is added by a seq-end callback.
Format a slice of `Cell` IR entries into a `.cell` text representation.
Format a slice of `Cell` IR entries with blank lines between each entry.
Block-level: parse a block's rows into a struct.
`BLOCK_NAME` is the case-insensitive block name used for lookup.
`BLOCK_ALIASES` provides alternative block names also accepted during deserialization.
The primary `BLOCK_NAME` is tried first; aliases are checked only if it is not found.
File-level: assemble a top-level struct from the full token slice.
Leaf-level: parse a single `CellValue` into a Rust type.
Implemented by scalars, unit enums, keyword enums, and row structs.
KeyValue-level: parse the value at a known key into a type.
`KEY_NAME` is the case-insensitive key used for lookup.
`KEY_ALIASES` provides alternative key names also accepted during deserialization.
Parse a `.cell` / `.param` file string and deserialize into `T`.
Find a block by name (case-insensitive). Returns its rows slice on success.
Find a block by trying multiple names in order (case-insensitive).
Returns the rows of the first matching block.
If no block matches any name, returns `Error::KeyNotFound` with the first name.
Find a key-value entry by key (case-insensitive). Returns reference to its CellValue.
Check if a Flag entry with the given name exists (case-insensitive).
Extract exactly N f64 values from a CellValue::Array row.
Extract exactly N i32 values from a CellValue::Array row.
Extract a bool from a scalar CellValue.
Extract an f64 from a scalar CellValue.
Extract an i32 from a scalar CellValue.
Extract a String from a CellValue::Str or CellValue::String.
Extract a u32 from a scalar CellValue.
Contains a list of k-points that define a path through reciprocal space for band structure calculations.
Represents a single point entry within the BS_KPOINT_PATH block.
Each entry contains three fractional k-point coordinates relative to the reciprocal space lattice vectors.
Specifies the spacing of k-points along the band structure path.
This data block contains a list of k-points at which the Brillouin zone
will be sampled during non-self consistent band structure calculations,
along with the associated weights. It has the same format as `KPOINTS_LIST`.
The first three entries on a line are the fractional positions of the
k-point relative to the reciprocal space lattice vectors.
The final entry on a line is the weight of the k-point relative to the
others specified. The sum of the weights must be equal to 1.
Contains a list of k-points and their weights for Brillouin zone sampling.
Specifies the Monkhorst-Pack grid parameters for generating SCF k-points.
Keyword type: Block (but actually a key-value with 3 integers)
Specifies the offset of the Monkhorst-Pack grid for SCF k-point sampling.
Specifies the spacing of k-points in the Monkhorst-Pack grid.
Contains a list of k-points and their weights for magnetic resonance calculations.
The first three entries on a line are the fractional positions of the
k-point relative to the reciprocal space lattice vectors.
The final entry on a line is the weight of the k-point relative to the
others specified. The sum of the weights must be equal to 1.
Contains a list of k-points and their weights for optical properties calculations.
The first three entries on a line are the fractional positions of the
k-point relative to the reciprocal space lattice vectors.
The final entry on a line is the weight of the k-point relative to the
others specified. The sum of the weights must be equal to 1.
Specifies the spacing of k-points along the spectral band structure path.
This data block contains a list of k-points at which the Brillouin zone
will be sampled during spectral calculations,
Specifies the Monkhorst-Pack grid for spectral k-point sampling.
Specifies the offset of the Monkhorst-Pack grid for spectral k-point sampling.
Specifies the spacing of k-points in the Monkhorst-Pack grid for spectral sampling.
Represents the constraints on lattice parameters (lengths and angles) during relaxation/MD.
Consists of two sets of indices:
- One for the lattice vector magnitudes (a, b, c)
- One for the lattice angles (alpha, beta, gamma)
Controls whether or not all of the lattice parameters remain fixed
Controls whether or not all of the ionic positions remain fixed
Controls whether or not the center of mass of the ions remains fixed
Default: If FIX_ALL_IONS : FALSE then the default value is TRUE.
Controls whether or not the volume of the cell remains fixed
during relaxation or molecular dynamics. Cell angles and cell lengths
Represents a single constraint entry within the IONIC_CONSTRAINTS block.
Ic CCC/Ic In Rix Riy Riz
- In: Ion number within the species
- Rix, Riy, Riz: Coefficients for x, y, z Cartesian coordinates
Defines linear constraints on changes to Cartesian ionic positions.
I1 CCC1/I1 In1 R1i R1j R1k
I2 CCC2/I2 In2 R2i R2j R2k
Represents a specific atom site, including its species, index within that species,
and the periodic image (Miller indices) it occupies.
Represents the type of internal coordinate constraint.
Represents a single nonlinear constraint entry within the NONLINEAR_CONSTRAINTS block.
Defines constrained internal coordinates (bonds, angles, torsions) that are
held fixed at the values of the initial structure.
Represents the electric field vector in Cartesian coordinates.
Default unit for the field vector: eV/Ang/e (if units are not specified).
Represents the external pressure tensor applied to the system.
Default unit for the tensor components: GPa (if units are not specified).
Default value: No external pressure (zero tensor).
This data block contains the cell lattice vectors in Cartesian coordinates. It has the following format:
Where R1x is the x-component of the first lattice vector, R2y is the y-component of the second lattice vector, and so on.
[units] specifies the units in which the lattice vectors are defined. If no units are given, the default of Å is used.
Contains a list of k-points and their weights for fine phonon calculations.
The first three entries on a line are the fractional positions of the
k-point relative to the reciprocal space lattice vectors.
The final entry on a line is the weight of the k-point relative to the
others specified. The sum of the weights must be equal to 1.
Contains a list of q-vectors that define a fine path through reciprocal space for phonon calculations.
Specifies the spacing of k-points along the phonon fine band structure path.
Specifies the fine Monkhorst-Pack grid parameters for phonon calculations.
Specifies the offset of the fine Monkhorst-Pack grid for phonon calculations.
Specifies the spacing of fine k-points in the Monkhorst-Pack grid for phonon calculations.
Represents a single direction entry within the PHONON_GAMMA_DIRECTIONS block.
Each entry contains three components of a direction vector for approaching the gamma point.
Contains a list of directions of approach to the gamma point, used to evaluate the non-analytical
part of the LO/TO phonon splitting.
Contains a list of k-points and their weights for phonon calculations.
The first three entries on a line are the fractional positions of the
k-point relative to the reciprocal space lattice vectors.
The final entry on a line is the weight of the k-point relative to the
others specified. The sum of the weights must be equal to 1.
Contains a list of q-vectors that define a path through reciprocal space for phonon calculations.
Represents a single point entry within the PHONON_KPOINT_PATH block.
Each entry contains three fractional q-vector coordinates relative to the reciprocal space lattice vectors.
Specifies the spacing of k-points along the phonon band structure path.
Specifies the offset of the Monkhorst-Pack grid for phonon k-point sampling.
Specifies the spacing of k-points for the Monkhorst-Pack phonon grid.
Contains a list of k-points and their weights for supercell calculations.
The first three entries on a line are the fractional positions of the
k-point relative to the reciprocal space lattice vectors.
The final entry on a line is the weight of the k-point relative to the
others specified. The sum of the weights must be equal to 1.
Represents a single atom entry within the POSITIONS_ABS block.
Consists of the element symbol/number, absolute coordinates, and optional spin/mixture qualifiers.
Contains a list of atomic positions in absolute (Cartesian) coordinates.
Species1/I1 R1x R1y R1z [SPIN S1] [MIXTURE M1 W1]
Species2/I2 R2x R2y R2z [SPIN S2] [MIXTURE M2 W2]
Represents a single atom entry within the POSITIONS_ABS_INTERMEDIATE block.
Consists of the element symbol/number, absolute coordinates, and optional spin/mixture qualifiers.
Contains a list of atomic positions in absolute (Cartesian) coordinates for intermediate structures.
Species1/I1 R1x R1y R1z [SPIN S1] [MIXTURE M1 W1]
Species2/I2 R2x R2y R2z [SPIN S2] [MIXTURE M2 W2]
Represents a single atom entry within the POSITIONS_ABS_PRODUCT block.
Consists of the element symbol/number, absolute coordinates, and optional spin/mixture qualifiers.
Contains a list of atomic positions in absolute (Cartesian) coordinates for product structures.
Species1/I1 R1x R1y R1z [SPIN S1] [MIXTURE M1 W1]
Species2/I2 R2x R2y R2z [SPIN S2] [MIXTURE M2 W2]
Represents a single atom entry within the POSITIONS_FRAC block.
Consists of the element symbol/number, fractional coordinates, and optional spin/mixture qualifiers.
Contains a list of atomic positions in fractional coordinates.
Contains a list of atomic positions in fractional coordinates for the intermediate
geometry in a transition state search calculation.
Contains a list of atomic positions in fractional coordinates for the product
geometry in a transition state search calculation.
Represents the species identifier for an atom in a `POSITIONS_*` block.
Can be either a chemical symbol (e.g., "Fe") or an atomic number (e.g., 26).
Represents the specification for Hubbard U values for a specific atom/ion.
Defines the Hubbard U values to use for specific orbitals.
Represents the unit for Hubbard U values.
Represents an orbital type and its associated Hubbard U value.
Defines the quantization (magnetization) axis for LDA+U calculations.
Default: The fractional coordinates equivalent to the c axis.
This input defines the quantization axis (1 1 -1) which is most appropriate
for a fcc structure (along one of the fourth order axes).
Defines customized parameters for semi-empirical dispersion corrections.
Represents a single entry within the SEDC_CUSTOM_PARAMS block.
Each entry specifies custom dispersion parameters for a species.
Represents a single parameter entry in SEDC_CUSTOM_PARAMS format.
Represents a single entry within the SPECIES_LCAO_STATES block,
linking a species to the number of LCAO states for it.
Defines the size of the LCAO basis set used for population analysis.
Defines the mass of each atomic species.
Represents a single entry within the SPECIES_MASS block,
linking a species to its mass.
Defines the pseudopotential files associated with each atomic species.
Represents a single entry within the SPECIES_POT block,
linking a species to its pseudopotential filename.
Defines the nuclear electric quadrupole moment of each atomic species.
Represents a single entry within the SPECIES_Q block,
linking a species to its quadrupole moment.
If present, indicates that symmetry should be generated rather than read from the input.
When SYMMETRY_GENERATE is present in the .cell file, CASTEP will generate symmetry
operations automatically from the unit cell geometry instead of reading them from
Represents a single symmetry operation, consisting of a 3x3 rotation matrix and a 3-element translation vector.
Contains a list of symmetry operations under which the unit cell is invariant.
Each operation consists of a 3x3 rotation matrix and a 3-element translation vector.
R11 R21 R31  <- Row 1 of rotation matrix for op 1
R12 R22 R32  <- Row 2 of rotation matrix for op 1
R13 R23 R33  <- Row 3 of rotation matrix for op 1
T1  T2  T3   <- Translation vector for op 1
R11 R21 R31  <- Row 1 of rotation matrix for op 2
Controls the tolerance within which symmetry will be considered to be satisfied.
If an ion is found within this distance of its symmetric position, the symmetry
will be considered to be satisfied. Unit of length must be specified.
Contains a list of ionic velocities in Cartesian coordinates.
Represents a single atom entry within the IONIC_VELOCITIES block.
Consists of the element symbol/number and velocity components in Cartesian coordinates.
Complete representation of a CASTEP `.cell` file.
This is the primary type for working with CASTEP cell files. It contains all
structural information, calculation parameters, and optional blocks that can
All other fields are optional and correspond to specific CASTEP features.
Use the builder pattern (via [`bon`](https://docs.rs/bon)) for ergonomic construction:
Implements [`FromCellFile`] for parsing and [`ToCellFile`] for serialization:
The document supports all standard CASTEP cell file blocks:
Lattice vector specification for the simulation cell.
Defines the periodic boundary conditions of the crystal structure.
Supports both Cartesian and ABC+angles representations.
// Use builder to construct LatticeCart, then wrap in enum
Atomic positions within the simulation cell.
Positions can be specified in either fractional (relative to lattice vectors)
or absolute Cartesian coordinates. CASTEP requires exactly one position block
- **Fractional**: Coordinates relative to lattice vectors (0.0 to 1.0 range).
Most common for periodic systems. Corresponds to `%BLOCK POSITIONS_FRAC`.
- **Absolute**: Cartesian coordinates in Angstroms. Useful for non-periodic
or mixed systems. Corresponds to `%BLOCK POSITIONS_ABS`.
// Use builder to construct PositionsFrac, then wrap in enum
Controls the tolerance for accepting convergence of a single eigenvalue or band.
Controls the maximum number of conjugate gradient steps taken for each electronic band.
Controls the maximum number of iterations to perform when calculating band structure.
Determines the number of bands at each k-point when performing a band structure calculation.
Default: NBANDS + 5√NBANDS (context-dependent, not directly representable here)
Controls the number of extra bands at each k-point in addition to the number of occupied bands.
Controls the percentage of extra bands at each k-point in addition to the number of occupied bands.
Determines whether or not to update the estimate of the Thomas-Fermi screening length.
Controls which functional is used to determine the exchange-correlation potential.
Band structure calculation parameters for CASTEP calculations
This group contains settings that control band structure calculations,
including convergence criteria, number of bands, and exchange-correlation functional.
Only one of `bs_nbands`, `bs_nextra_bands`, or `bs_perc_extra_bands` may be specified.
Specifies the derivative of total energy with respect to the natural log
of the basis cutoff energy for manual finite basis set correction.
Default: 0.0 (but requires a value if FINITE_BASIS_CORR : 1)
Specifies the precision of the basis set by choosing the level of convergence
of atomic energies with respect to the plane wave cutoff energy.
Specifies the cutoff energy for the plane wave basis sets.
Default: The value associated with the FINE level of accuracy for the pseudopotentials.
Determines the maximum size of the g-vectors included in the fine grid.
Default: -1 1/bohr (results in fine and standard grids being identical)
Determines the maximum size of the g-vectors included in the fine grid
Default: 1.0 (results in fine and standard grids being identical)
Determines whether or not to apply a finite basis set correction
to energy and stress when cell parameters change.
Controls the number of points used to estimate the BASIS_DE_DLOGE
in automatic calculation of the finite basis set correction.
Determines the spacing of cutoff energies used to estimate the BASIS_DE_DLOGE
in automatic calculation of the finite basis set correction.
Determines whether a fixed number of plane waves (TRUE) or a fixed
plane wave cutoff energy (FALSE) will be used when doing a variable cell calculation.
Determines the size of the standard grid, relative to the diameter
Basis set parameters for CASTEP calculations
This group contains settings that control the basis set and grid,
including cutoff energy, grid scaling, and finite basis corrections.
Determines the mixing amplitude for the charge density in the density mixing procedure.
Determines the maximum g-vector at which the charge density is mixed.
Determines the cutoff energy for the densities used in the density mixing scheme.
Determines the maximum number of densities to store in the history used during the density mixing procedure.
Determines the weighting factor for the densities used in the density mixing scheme.
Default: -1 (CASTEP will automatically select the appropriate value)
Determines the mixing amplitude for the spin density in the density mixing procedure.
Determines the maximum g-vector at which the spin density is mixed.
Determines which mixing scheme will be used in the density mixing procedure.
Density Mixing parameters for CASTEP calculations
This group contains settings that control the charge density mixing scheme
used during self-consistent field (SCF) iterations, including mixing amplitudes,
cutoff energies, and history length for various mixing algorithms.
Controls whether to compute the zero-frequency dielectric permittivity based on ionic response.
Specifies which non-linear optical property to calculate during a TASK=EFIELD calculation.
Determines the size of the convergence window during a linear response calculation.
Controls the tolerance for accepting convergence of the field constants.
Specifies how many of the lowest lying modes to ignore for ionic permittivity/polarizability.
Controls the maximum number of conjugate gradient steps during a linear response calculation.
Controls the maximum number of iterations in a linear response to electric field calculation.
Electric Field parameters for CASTEP calculations
This group contains settings that control linear response to electric field calculations,
including convergence criteria, maximum iterations, and nonlinear response options.
Specifies the total charge of the system.
Determines the maximum number of bands at any k-point and spin.
Default: Calculated based on NELECTRONS/NUP+NDOWN and NEXTRA_BANDS/PERC_EXTRA_BANDS
Determines the total number of down-spin electrons.
If SPIN is specified: NDOWN = (NELECTRONS - SPIN) / 2
Specifies the total number of electrons in the system.
If CHARGE is specified, NELECTRONS is chosen to match the charge.
If NUP and NDOWN are specified, NELECTRONS = NUP + NDOWN.
Otherwise, a default value for a neutral system is chosen.
Controls the number of extra bands in addition to the number of occupied bands.
Determines the total number of up-spin electrons.
If SPIN is specified: NUP = (NELECTRONS + SPIN) / 2
Controls the percentage of extra bands at each k-point in addition to the number of occupied bands.
Controls whether to apply semi-empirical dispersion correction.
Damping parameter for G06 dispersion correction.
Damping parameter for JCHS dispersion correction.
Damping parameter for TS dispersion correction.
Lambda parameter for OBS dispersion correction.
N parameter for OBS dispersion correction.
Scaling parameter for G06 dispersion correction.
Scaling parameter for JCHS dispersion correction.
Specifies the semi-empirical dispersion correction scheme to use.
Short-range scaling parameter for JCHS dispersion correction.
Short-range scaling parameter for TS dispersion correction.
Determines the initial value for the number of unpaired electrons
0.0 when the total number of electrons is even.
1.0 when the total number of electrons is odd.
Defines the type of electronic spectroscopy calculation to perform.
Number of excited states to calculate in TDDFT.
Selects which method to use for the position operator in TDDFT.
Index of the excited state to calculate properties for in TDDFT.
Electronic Excitations parameters for CASTEP calculations
This group contains settings that control excited state calculations,
including TDDFT and spectral property calculations.
Controls the tolerance for accepting convergence of the Fermi-energy.
Determines the size of the convergence window during a electronic minimization run.
Determines the name of the file into which wavefunction and density data are written.
Controls the tolerance for accepting convergence of a single eigenvalue during density mixing minimization.
Default: The lower of 1e-6 eV and ELEC_ENERGY_TOL*NATOMS/NBANDS
Controls the tolerance for accepting convergence of the total energy in an electronic minimization.
Specifies the name of the file from which wavefunction and density data should be restored.
Controls the method used to minimize electronic states.
Specifies whether or not the occupancies of the bands should be fixed.
Determines the maximum number of conjugate gradient steps in an SCF cycle.
If ELECTRONIC_MINIMIZER is not defined, the default is 4.
Determines the maximum number of SCF cycles performed in an electronic minimization.
Determines the maximum number of steepest descent steps in an SCF cycle.
If ELECTRONIC_MINIMIZER is not defined, the default is 1.
Determines the electronic minimization method used in the self-consistent calculation.
Default: MetalsMethod::Edft (or MetalsMethod::Dm if FIX_OCCUPANCY is FALSE)
Specifies the number of SCF cycles between updates to the wavefunction and density data file.
Determines the width of the Fermi-surface smearing.
Determines the number of electronic iterations for which the total spin is fixed.
Electronic minimisation parameters for CASTEP calculations
This group contains settings that control the electronic minimization process,
including SCF convergence criteria, minimizer selection, and smearing options.
Electronic structure parameters for CASTEP calculations
This group contains settings that control electronic structure calculations,
including charge, spin, band structure, and semi-empirical dispersion corrections (SEDC).
Determines the averaging scheme for estimating the Thomas-Fermi screening length.
Note: NLXC_K_SCRN_AVERAGING_SCHEME is the latest form; K_SCRN_AVERAGING_SCHEME is supported for backward compatibility.

Controls paging of exchange potential in non-local exchange-correlation.

Controls whether or not different wavefunctions are used for different spins.

Options for fine control (SUCCESS=, FAILURE=, BACKUP=).

This group contains settings that control molecular dynamics simulations,

Phonon calculation parameters for CASTEP calculations

Population Analysis parameters for CASTEP calculations
This group contains settings that control population analysis calculations,

Transition State parameters for CASTEP calculations
This group contains settings that control transition state search calculations,

Complete representation of a CASTEP `.param` file.
This is the primary type for working with CASTEP parameter files. It organizes
the 100+ CASTEP parameters into 18 logical groups, making it easier to discover
related parameters and maintain the codebase.
Each field represents a group of related parameters. All groups default to empty
(all parameters `None`), allowing you to specify only the parameters you need.
The document automatically validates inter-group constraints during parsing:
- Band count parameters (`NBANDS`, `NEXTRA_BANDS`, `PERC_EXTRA_BANDS`) are mutually exclusive
- Band structure parameters (`BS_NBANDS`, `BS_NEXTRA_BANDS`, `BS_PERC_EXTRA_BANDS`) are mutually exclusive
Each parameter group also validates its own internal constraints.
Or use [`Default`] for an empty document:
